markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 187 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- This deep red cloak fits lightly about your shoulders, and the edges perpetually twitch slightly, as though caught in a breeze. The cloth feels smoother than silk, rippling and swaying like liquid when in motion. You feel a lively energy infusing your arms and legs. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15), and a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against attacks from reactions triggered by your movement. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An enemy misses you with a melee Strike; **Effect** You slip around the attacking creature with ease. You Step, without moving away from the triggering enemy, and then make a melee Strike against the triggering enemy if it's within reach. If you do make a Strike, the target attempts a DC 38 Perception check before you roll. **Failure** This creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the Strike.
**Critical Failure** This creature is flat-footed against all your attacks until the end of their next turn. **Activate** command, envision ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The cloak hums with power as your whirl it around yourself, disappearing amid a brief flash of light. Teleport up to double your Speed to a location you can see. At the end of the teleportation, you can make a melee Strike against a creature within reach, if there is one.
name: Mercurial Mantle

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 189 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn belt **Bulk** 1 --- The thin, glittering strands of rope that comprise this thick belt appear to be spun gold. Strung along the front of the belt are a collection of four multicolored, perfectly spherical beads: jade, turquoise, quartz, and amethyst. While wearing the lash, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13). When you invest the belt, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** command, Interact; **Effect** You touch one of the jewels affixed to the sage's lash and speak a command word. Depending on the jewel, a different effect is produced that affects you and all living creatures in a 30-foot emanation. After the effect occurs, all four jewels disappear from the lash, reappearing at the next dawn.
  • **Jade** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) Speak “purity” to produce a wave of green light that ends any [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition and any [ongoing poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) affecting creatures in the emanation.
  • **Turquoise** Speak “clarity” to grant all affected a +2 status bonus to their next Will save for 1 minute.
  • **Quartz** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) Speak “lenity” to have each creature regain 30 Hit Points.
  • **Amethyst** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=138)) Speak “verity” to emit a magical trail of light from the lash that reveals the location of each creature in the emanation. For 1 round, if they would be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), they become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) instead, and if they would be [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) or [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by a creature, they're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) instead. A creature can attempt a DC 43 [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to avoid the lash's detection.
name: Sage's Lash

>
[Specialist's Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1077)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each _specialist's ring_ is dedicated to a single school of magic, and the ring is covered in symbols and glyphs related to that school according to the creator's arcane studies. A _specialist's ring_ has the trait corresponding to its school of magic. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) checks, and a +1 circumstance bonus to recognize magical effects and items of the specific school of magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a wizard school spell of the corresponding school. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Specialist's Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stampede Medallion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1080)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you invest this medallion for your eidolon, it changes shape to appear as a tiny bejeweled facsimile of the eidolon, magically attached just over your eidolon's heart. While your eidolon wears the medallion, they gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your eidolon Concentrates on the medallion and their connection to you, allowing them to momentarily manifest into a stampede of dozens of copies of themself. The stampede rampages out in every direction, swerving around your allies while trampling any foe on the ground in an emanation around your eidolon with a radius equal to your eidolon's Speed. Each of these foes takes 8d6 bludgeoning damage, with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. On a critical failure, the foe is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). After dealing damage, the stampede of eidolons vanishes as quickly as it appeared.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Warfare Lore checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Blade Thesis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1081)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 191

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This collection of lacquered rice paper scrolls mounted on flexible bamboo contains a wealth of calligraphic essays and paintings on the art of war, specifically focused on the use of various weapons in warfare and how to tactically deploy warriors using those weapons to the best possible advantage. Consulting the thesis grants a +2 item bonus to [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks. Like most scholarly compendiums, this usage requires holding the thesis in one hand.

 The thesis also serves as an extradimensional armory for weapons and ammunition. The thesis has a capacity of 5 Bulk, and only weapons and ammunition can be stored within it. You and others can Interact with the thesis to store or retrieve a weapon or piece of ammunition in it, like a mundane container.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _thousand-blade thesis_ dramatically unfurls, and the weapons contained within it spring forth and array themselves impressively in the air, floating within easy reach. For 1 minute, you can use a free action to Interact to draw one of the floating weapons. Others can attempt to nab them out of the air, but to do so they must critically succeed at a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) check. You can't place weapons back into the thesis until the minute elapses.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thousand-Blade Thesis

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 191 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** worn gloves **Bulk** L --- These bronze gauntlets each have a small red gem embedded in the wrist. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35). If you successfully Grapple an enemy that's at least one size category larger than you, the gauntlets dig into it, dealing bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier, plus an additional 2d6 on a critical success. When you invest the gloves, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** Interact ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You have two hands free; **Effect** You clap the gauntlets together with a thunderous crack that deals 6d10 sonic damage in a 30-foot emanation. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is deafened for 1 minute, and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
name: Titan's Grasp

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Crown of the Companion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1086)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 75, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn crown

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stories tell of a forgotten king who once loved his subjects so much he was willing to give his own life energy for them. Whether true or not, this majestic wooden crown bears elaborate carvings depicting that tale. It's ringed with images of the same kingly figure giving more and more of himself to a throng of needy subjects. While wearing it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You bow to an ally within 30 feet, creating a magical bond between the two of you. You cast [_shield other_](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) targeting the ally. The link remains even if you move more than 30 feet away from them. At the end of the spell's duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Nosoi Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1095)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Night of the Gray Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Nosois serve as scribes and messengers to psychopomps. They are rare outside of the Boneyard, so it's common for [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) venturing to other planes to carry a nosoi facsimile to aid in their travels. This tiny icon of a masked raven emits twittering sounds in perfect accompaniment to your performances. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to play an instrument, orate, or sing. You can cast [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) once per day as a divine innate spell. 

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You twist the base of the charm, and the twittering it emits becomes helpful words to boost your arguments. You lose the item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to make [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) until you use this activation again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) as an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet for 1 hour as long as you continue to hold the charm. This allows you to sense the life force that animates living creatures and the perverse force that animates the dead, though you can't distinguish between the two.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Performance checks when played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a haunt or incorporeal undead
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit-Singer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1138)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Etheric spirit-singers create an eerie spectral sound based on the presence of spiritual essence and other ethereal energies. All spirit-singers can be used as musical instruments by manipulating the sensitivity to ethereal energies. However, they have an even greater benefit in areas heavy with spiritual essence. When played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) or an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), a spirit-singer grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. While playing a spirit-singer, you also gain a +1 item bonus to checks to detect a haunt or incorporeal undead, and you can roll a check to notice a haunt even if you aren't actively [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) for it, due to the distortions of the spirit-singer's music. A haunt or incorporeal undead that is intelligent enough to notice the effects it is having on the spirit-singer's music and that can't otherwise communicate with the living might choose to use the spirit-singer to do so if it wishes. For instance, it could try to guide the spirit-singer player towards a location by creating distortions in that direction or, if it understands language, it could try to answer questions by creating one distortion for yes and two distortions for no. Unless stated otherwise in its usage entry, a spirit-singer functions like a heavy [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37); rather than carrying it, the musician places the spirit-singer in a particular position and uses both hands to play.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spirit-Singer
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 16

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spirit-Singer (Handheld)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spirit-Singer (Incredible)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 16

</row>

</column>

---

This spirit-singer is fine-tuned to the point that you can analyze its song more intricately. Increase the item bonus to Performance checks near spiritual disturbances, and the item bonus to detecting haunts and incorporeal undead to +2. While playing the spirit-singer, you can notice haunts that normally require you to have a master proficiency in Perception to detect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spirit-Singer (Incredible Handheld)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

This spirit-singer functions just like an incredible spirit-singer, except for its Usage and Bulk.

item_bonus_note: Performance checks when played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a haunt or incorporeal undead
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit-Singer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1138)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Etheric spirit-singers create an eerie spectral sound based on the presence of spiritual essence and other ethereal energies. All spirit-singers can be used as musical instruments by manipulating the sensitivity to ethereal energies. However, they have an even greater benefit in areas heavy with spiritual essence. When played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) or an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), a spirit-singer grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. While playing a spirit-singer, you also gain a +1 item bonus to checks to detect a haunt or incorporeal undead, and you can roll a check to notice a haunt even if you aren't actively [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) for it, due to the distortions of the spirit-singer's music. A haunt or incorporeal undead that is intelligent enough to notice the effects it is having on the spirit-singer's music and that can't otherwise communicate with the living might choose to use the spirit-singer to do so if it wishes. For instance, it could try to guide the spirit-singer player towards a location by creating distortions in that direction or, if it understands language, it could try to answer questions by creating one distortion for yes and two distortions for no. Unless stated otherwise in its usage entry, a spirit-singer functions like a heavy [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37); rather than carrying it, the musician places the spirit-singer in a particular position and uses both hands to play.

item_bonus_note: Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Violet Ray](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1139)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 83

**Price** 1,893 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One of the many wondrous devices brought into the world by Stasian technology is the seemingly miraculous violet ray. Physicians claim it anything from headaches to heartburn, or nausea to deafness, all with an easy and painless treatment. The device is a glass vacuum with an insulated handle connected to a small Stasian coil. When powered, the glass tube fills with purple light and becomes warm to the touch. Pressing the tube to one's body is said to increase blood flow, eliminate toxins, and many other beneficial effects. A violet ray functions as a set of [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) and provides a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54), [Treat Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=55), [Treat Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=56), or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You apply the violet ray to an adjacent creature and attempt to counteract the [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) level of 6 and a counteract modifier of +22, using the source of the condition to determine the condition's counteract level and DC. If the condition was caused by an ongoing effect and you don't remove that effect, the condition returns after 1 minute. Each use of this ability can only counteract a single condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Violet Ray

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aromatic Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1188)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 168

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The components of this ammunition emit a strong smell when combined during its activation. A creature hit by an activated aromatic ammunition gains a distinct odor for up to 1 hour or until the scent is washed off (requiring at least a gallon of water and 10 minutes of scrubbing). Any creatures within 30 feet smell the target, allowing even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) doubles the range at which they can detect the target using their scent compared to the normal range of their scent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aromatic Ammunition

item_bonus_note: Stealth checks while you attempt to Hide or Sneak in the specific environment it has been prepared for
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1201)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lightweight mesh fits easily over light armor. The suit is designed to incorporate local flora and ground clutter into the mesh to help you blend in seamlessly with the environment. Due to the abrasive nature of the materials used, this item is unsuitable for use by unarmored characters. You can prepare the suit for use within your current environment by using an exploration activity that takes at least 10 minutes, but sometimes longer if the materials are hard to find or the environment is unusual enough to warrant additional difficulty in preparing camouflage that can blend with it consistently.

 A suit prepared in this manner grants you a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks while you attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) in the specific environment it has been prepared for. The suit remains usable in this manner until you rest for the night, though it doesn't grant the benefit whenever you aren't in the appropriate environment. The GM might rule that some environments are unusual enough that you can't create a camouflage suit appropriate for the environment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of dim light visible through the scope (as well as in areas of darkness, if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Darkvision Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1212)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of dim light visible through the scope (as well as in areas of darkness, if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetite Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1213)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 181

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

More of a field projector than a scope, this small cylinder of magnetite has been enchanted to spread and accelerate the shrapnel of a [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) weapon further than the weapon's natural capabilities. Furthermore, some of the floating magnetite inside the scope's structure can help you when attempting to determine the your allies' locations. The magnetite scope grants you a +2 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) when using the scope to assist you in navigating. This scope can only be attached to firearms with the scatter trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** The next Strike you make this round with the gun to which the scope is attached has its scatter radius increased by 5 feet.

</title>

<traits>

**Bulk** L

---

These scopes use magically enhanced lenses to extend the range of your weapon and help spot distant foes. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) creatures through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)    **Effect** While gazing through the scope, you zoom in on your targets to make it easier to hit them at a distance. You increase the [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of the weapon to which the scope is attached by 5 feet until the beginning of your next turn or until you're no longer wielding the weapon, whichever comes first.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scope of Truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1216)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Replace the word "transmutation" in the scope of truth with the word "polymorph."_

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _scope of truth_ is a bit bigger than most to accommodate larger lenses, which have been magically prepared with tiny shards from crystal balls to reveal the truth. The scope grants you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks made to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) in areas you can see through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can see things through the scope as they actually are. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a +20 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 7 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) or [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it, not to end the spell or effect. For instance, if the check succeeds against a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) spell, you can see the creature's true form, but you don't end the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Truth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diving Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1261)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Diving suits are bulky, waterproofed leather outfits with copper helmets, worn by divers and underwater salvagers. The suit grants a +1 item bonus to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) checks made underwater, and the helmet features tubes for connecting [_bottled air_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=250). When connected to a diving suit, bottled air doesn't need to be held and can be used to breathe as a free action. If you wear armor over a diving suit, you become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until you remove the diving suit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diving Suit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonfly Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1272)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 26

**Price** 310 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your eyes transform into those of a giant dragonfly, the thousands of separate facets wrapping around your head, and a pair of long, delicate, insectile wings grow from your upper back. You gain a fly Speed equal to your land Speed. Additionally, you gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416) and a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks, and you can't be flanked except by creatures higher level than you are (though lower-level creatures can still help their higher-level allies flank). These effects last for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonfly Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drum of Upheaval](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1281)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy drum is engraved along the sides with images of centaurs in fierce combat. The drum grants you a +3 item bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks you make using the drum. Additionally, it imparts the rhythms of two songs upon your mind as soon as you touch it. One is a fast marching cadence; the other is a frenetic ritual dance. Each song has a different activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Dozens of spectral centaurs burst from the drum and stampede in a 60-foot cone. The stampede deals 7d10 force damage (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The drum casts a DC 43 [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [centaur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=76).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Drum of Upheaval

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silhouette Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1306)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 43

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In bright light, this dark cloak shimmers with patches of color that shift and move even when the cloth is still. In dim light or darkness, the cloak seems to blend into your surroundings and grants you a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact (1 minute);     **Effect** You and objects you wear or carry fall into your shadow, becoming a two-dimensional silhouette. In this form you aren't incorporeal, but you also don't have a solid form and don't occupy your space, allowing other creatures to pass through or end their turn there. You gain resistance 20 to all damage except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage, damage from effects with the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) trait, and damage from Strikes with the [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune. This increases to resistance 40 against nonmagical damage. Other creatures can't attempt Strength-based skill checks against you. Your land and climb Speeds both become 40 feet. You can't use actions except [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), Step, or Stride actions using the granted Speeds or an Interact activation to return to your normal form. If your current space doesn't have sufficient room for you to return to your normal form, you return to your normal form in the nearest open space.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take damage;  **Effect** You fold the cloak over yourself and vanish into your shadow for a moment, gaining the benefits of the first activation until the end of the current creature's turn. The damage resistance applies to the triggering damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silhouette Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon's Blood Pudding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1310)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While some unscrupulous chefs claim that this savory pudding is made with real dragon's blood, its crimson color and acrid smell actually come from bloody mandrake paste, ginger root, and distilled terrap sap. This potent combination singes the nostrils and throat, removing effects that make you sluggish. When you consume the pudding, it attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to remove the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition from a single source, using the source of that condition to determine the counteract level and DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +6 counteract modifier.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +9 counteract modifier. You also become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round. While taking the additional action from being quickened, you scream, as a result of both the flavor and the mandrake paste's properties. You can use the additional action to Step, Stride, Strike, or [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). If you choose to Demoralize, you gain a +2 item bonus to the [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +20 counteract modifier. You become quickened as per the 5th-level version, except you gain a +3 item bonus to the Intimidation check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +31 counteract modifier. You become quickened as per the 5th-level version, except the effect lasts for 2 rounds and you gain a +4 item bonus to the Intimidation check.
name: Dragon's Blood Pudding (Moderate)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impossible Cake](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1312)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) “Type of item” refers to a broad category of items such as books, spells, etc, not a specific object._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sleight-of-hand for the taste buds is flavored with absinthe and honey and always resembles something completely unlike a cake, from a pile of armor to a bowl of soup. Eating the cake gives you the confidence to make the impossible seem possible: for 10 minutes after the meal, you gain a +2 item bonus to your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46), as well as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to convince others that you possess knowledge about the type of item that the cake resembles.

</column>

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

pfs="Standard"
>
Impossible Cake (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

<traits>

This sleight-of-hand for the taste buds is flavored with absinthe and honey and always resembles something completely unlike a cake, from a pile of armor to a bowl of soup. Eating the cake gives you the confidence to make the impossible seem possible: for 10 minutes after the meal, you gain a +2 item bonus to your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46), as well as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to convince others that you possess knowledge about the type of item that the cake resembles.

>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The type of animal tongue used in this tomato-based stew shifts from region to region, but it's always cooked with spirit of wine and seasoned with [refined pesh](/Equipment.aspx?ID=626). Eating this restaurant-staff favorite empowers you to speak and understand a single language for 1 hour. This must be a language the stew's cook could speak and understand, chosen at the time of the stew's creation. It also helps your words land smoothly, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks made in that language for the same duration. It doesn't allow you to read the newly acquired language in its written form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pickpocket's Tailoring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1320)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** sewn into clothing

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Pickpocket's tailoring modifies an existing outfit by adding concealed interior pockets and strategically opened seams for hiding small objects inside the lining. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) of light Bulk or less in the pockets. When you get a failure (but not a critical failure, which works as normal) on a Stealth check to Conceal such an Object, observers know you're concealing an object somewhere, but they don't find the object unless they succeed at a DC 20 Perception check to locate the seams in the garment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pickpocket's Tailoring

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quick Wig](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _quick wig_ magically conceals your natural hair while worn, eliminating the need to braid or pin your hair beneath it. In addition, your facial hair and eyebrows change color to match the wig while you wear it. When used as part of a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) to go unrecognized by changing your hair, you no longer require a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), you gain a +1 item bonus on the check, and you reduce the time to create the disguise from 10 minutes to 5 minutes. You still need a disguise kit and the full time if you're using cosmetics and other props to change other aspects of your disguise or Impersonating a specific person, and the wig only provides its item bonus when Impersonating a specific person if that person's hair color and style match the wig's. _Quick wigs_ detangle themselves while not in use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Quick Wig

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wardrobe Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1323)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large stone eye is the size of a fist and decorated with elaborate carvings of robes and other garments.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;     **Effect** You enter a brief trance in which you envision yourself entering a large dressing room. You maneuver through the endless dressing-room mindscape and search for whatever articles of clothing, accessories, and other garments you desire. After the activation, you gain the effects of [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), except that it only causes you to appear to be wearing the exact outfit you selected within the mindscape. The illusion also disguises the _wardrobe stone_ to match your outfit and not appear out of place, such as by appearing to be a glove or bracelet, though it continues to occupy your hand while you're under the effects of _illusory disguise_. You can actively Dismiss the Spell, but the effects are also dismissed immediately when you're no longer holding the _wardrobe stone_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to a specific [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill associated with your outfit, such as Carpentry Lore when wearing a carpenter's outfit or Cooking Lore when wearing a chef's outfit.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 55,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain +3 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight and a +3 item bonus to a specific Lore skill associated with your outfit.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;     **Effect** You cast _prying eye_.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (10 minutes) envision, Interact;     **Effect** The wardrobe stone splits open and permanently transforms into a specific type of magical robe of your choosing. It can transform into any 20th-level or lower magical robe to which you have access, except for items that can't normally be [Crafted](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), such as [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253). This process is irreversible.
name: Wardrobe Stone (Moderate)

---

A nigh-infinite assortment of games exist in Golarion. Simple games, including dice, a deck of cards, or dominoes, cost 5 sp. Board games vary in cost from 1 gp for dexterity games like Bungle, 3 gp for colorful children's games like Cauldron Quest, and 5 gp for complex strategy games like Kingmaker and Abendego Raiders. Lavish game sets can cost much more than these prices, as they are made of expensive components and are intricately crafted works of art unto themselves.

pfs="Standard"
>
Loaded Dice
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 58

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

These dice are weighted on one side to ensure they always land with the desired number facing up. Loaded dice grant you a +1 item bonus to [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks to gamble with the dice. You can determine a die is loaded while handling it with a successful secret DC 20 Perception check. Determining dice are loaded without handling the dice is more difficult, especially since smart cheaters switch loaded dice in and out so they don't suspiciously roll the same number over and over. In this case, an observer must succeed at a secret Perception check against the roller's Games Lore or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) DC to notice the switches or the patterns.
name: Loaded Dice

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Maestro's Chair](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1357)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents also have access to all assistive items from Morhen’s Mobility Apparel_

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 6
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _maestro's chair_ is a [traveler's chair](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1356) with the [impulse control](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1361) upgrade and a small pipe organ installed into the frame. You can use the maestro's chair to make [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks in the same manner that you could use any mundane instrument, using both of your hands. When you make a Performance check using your chair, you gain a +1 item bonus to the check.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You use your chair to Stride up to your chair's speed. If you have a [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=31) cantrip that is currently active, you Sustain it as a free action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Maestro's Chair

item_bonus_note: When faking an injury, the blood pack grants a +2 item bonus to relevant Deception checks, such as to Lie about being injured
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fake Blood Pack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1395)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 92

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn under light armor or clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Adventurers have found a number of uses for these animal blood–filled bladders, which were originally used in theatrical productions. Whenever you take slashing or piercing damage with the fake blood pack under your clothes or armor, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, the blood pack is punctured. You or an ally can puncture the hidden pack intentionally. When faking an injury, the blood pack grants a +2 item bonus to relevant [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks, such as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) about being injured. Abilities that trigger when a creature deals bleed damage, determine if a creature is bleeding, or are otherwise based on bleed damage don't trigger or apply for blood from a fake blood pack, which might mean creatures with such abilities automatically realize the ruse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fake Blood Pack

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You think hard on a topic and receive a sudden inspiration. You attempt to Recall Knowledge using Lore. On this check, you roll twice and take the higher result.

item_bonus_note: Diplomacy checks while interacting with dragons of the same kind as the pendant's scale
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dragon Rune Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1442)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracelet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This polished golden bracelet is formed around the scale of a famous dragon. The bracelet is etched with esoteric symbols or words—always in [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)—that indicate kinship with dragons. As many types of _dragon rune bracelets_ exist as there are types of dragons, though dragon rune bracelets associated with [Uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or [Rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) dragons are Rare.

 While wearing a dragon rune bracelet, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks while interacting with dragons of the same type as the pendant's scale (such as [red dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) or [cloud dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176)). You also gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) effects from any kind of [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You can cast bloodline spells from the [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5), such as from the [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) class or [sorcerer multiclass archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=11); **Effect** You regain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a sorcerer bloodline spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of your turn, it is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Rune Bracelet

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Exploration Lens](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1443)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _exploration lens_ is a magical glass lens designed for a [bull's-eye lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29). When light from the lantern passes through the lens, it alters the light to make it easier to perform a common exploration activity with the lantern. You can find more information about exploration activities related to these lenses on page 496 of the Core Rulebook. Alternatively, an _exploration lens_ can be installed in a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) like an [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). In this case, light (such as that from a lantern, [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), or [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell) must still pass through the lens and you must hold the _wayfinder_ in your hand for it to function.

 There are several different types of exploration lens, each granting benefits with a different type of exploration activity. Each type of lens is distinguishable from all the other types by holding it up to a bright light.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Detecting)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When held up to light, this lens sparkles as though infused with glitter. While [Detecting Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=513) in exploration mode, when the lantern's light falls upon something you detect as magical, it pulsates faintly. This makes it easier to triangulate the magic, since you can use the cone of the bull's-eye lantern instead of the circular pulse of [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66). You also gain a +2 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) within the light.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Investigating)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The outer rim of this lens glows faintly when held up to the light. While activated, the lens grants you a +1 item bonus to skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) by outlining potential clues in a way that triggers your memory. If you're Investigating something while using the lens, you can always roll a Recall Knowledge skill check appropriate to the investigation for initiative.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Limited"
>
Exploration Lens (Searching)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This lens has an opalescent sheen when held up to light. The lens subtly outlines hidden doors and concealed hazards, granting you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to spot such features while [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519). Even if you aren't Searching, you get a check to find [traps](/Traits.aspx?ID=158) that normally require you to Search.
name: Exploration Lens (Detecting)

>
[Spiderfoot Brew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1461)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105), [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gelatinous, sticky fluid is made from the silk glands of giant spiders. When you drink a spiderfoot brew, tiny clinging hairs grow on your hands and feet, granting you a climb Speed and an item bonus to Athletics checks made to climb for the listed duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These wire spectacles have been fitted with circular glass lenses. While wearing the spectacles, you gain a +1 item bonus to Decipher Writing. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are attempting to read something in a language you don't understand; **Effect** You flick a small toggle on the side of the spectacles, flipping the lenses over in the frames. You can understand the meaning of the language you are reading. This doesn't let you understand codes, riddles, or metaphors (subject to the GM's discretion). This effect lasts 1 hour.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Grit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1494)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 391

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First developed in the Puddles about 20 years ago, this stimulant has grown from a diversion of the wretched poor to a habit for well- heeled merchants and nobles. At low doses, grit provides the physical body with increased energy while stimulating the idle mind into effortless flights of creative fancy. Abuse of the drug leads to severe hallucinations, numbing, and uncontrollable rage. Grit is produced by grinding down alchemically-enhanced barbarian chew, which is mixed with toxic plant- based substances, such as bitterbark or redroot. The powder is very expensive and highly addictive.

The save for addiction to grit is DC 15, and the addiction has the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

 **Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 4 hours; **Stage 1** +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks (10 minutes); **Stage 2** +1 item bonus to Athletics, Crafting, and Performance checks, –2 item penalty to Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 3** –4 item penalty to Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 4** as stage 3 (1 hour); **Stage 5** [confusion](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) (1 round); **Stage 6** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (remaining duration)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Demon Dust](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1495)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Inhaled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=96" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 391

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This highly addictive drug is produced from the crushed bones of [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28). Demon dust strengthens the inhaler's body for acts requiring great physical stamina. While the drug is in effect, users typically experience some form of memory loss. Others claim to have experienced hallucinations, flashbacks, and confusion for a few hours after the drug wears off.

The save for addiction to demon dust is DC 24, and the addiction has the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

 **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 8 hours; **Stage 1** +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and treat up to 4 Bulk as being negligible in weight (1 minute); **Stage 2** +2 item bonus to Athletics checks, treat up to 4 Bulk as being negligible in weight, and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 hour); **Stage 3** stupefied 1 (1 hour); **Stage 4** stupefied 2 (1 hour); **Stage 5** stupefied 2 and all memories made since you were first exposed to this dose of demon dust become hazy and vague (1 hour); **Stage 6** stupefied 2 and all memories made in the previous 24 hours become hazy and vague (1 hour)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Demon Dust

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Broken Tusk Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1508)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The followers of the Broken Tusk have passed down their custom of crafting and gifting these small ivory necklaces for generations. They're typically made from mammoth tusk (though any type of ivory will do), and each is carved to resemble a particular kind of animal—usually a raven, a moose, an ox, or an otter. Because tradition dictates that Broken Tusk followers take ivory only from already-dead animals, _Broken Tusk pendants_ are especially rare; if an individual loses their pendant, it might be years before they acquire the materials necessary to fashion a replacement. _Broken Tusk pendants_ bear no magical powers if they are made for oneself; only gifted pendants have true magical properties.

 You wear a _Broken Tusk pendant_ like jewelry or tie it to your clothing. A _Broken Tusk pendant_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks; this bonus increases to +2 when you [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) of the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) targets you with a melee attack, and you can see the animal;  **Effect** You gain a +1 item bonus to AC against the triggering attack, or a +2 item bonus if the animal is the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Broken Tusk Pendant
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Limited"
>
Broken Tusk Pendant (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The pendant grants a +2 item bonus to Nature checks (+3 to Command an Animal of the type depicted by the pendant). Once per day, when you use the _Broken Tusk pendant's_ activation, if the animal misses you, the _Broken Tusk pendant_ casts [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on the animal (DC 30). You can choose whether to use this once-per-day effect after the animal misses you.
name: Broken Tusk Pendant

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Broken Tusk Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1508)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The followers of the Broken Tusk have passed down their custom of crafting and gifting these small ivory necklaces for generations. They're typically made from mammoth tusk (though any type of ivory will do), and each is carved to resemble a particular kind of animal—usually a raven, a moose, an ox, or an otter. Because tradition dictates that Broken Tusk followers take ivory only from already-dead animals, _Broken Tusk pendants_ are especially rare; if an individual loses their pendant, it might be years before they acquire the materials necessary to fashion a replacement. _Broken Tusk pendants_ bear no magical powers if they are made for oneself; only gifted pendants have true magical properties.

 You wear a _Broken Tusk pendant_ like jewelry or tie it to your clothing. A _Broken Tusk pendant_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks; this bonus increases to +2 when you [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) of the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) targets you with a melee attack, and you can see the animal;  **Effect** You gain a +1 item bonus to AC against the triggering attack, or a +2 item bonus if the animal is the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Once per day, when you use the _Broken Tusk pendant's_ activation, if the animal misses you, the _Broken Tusk pendant_ casts [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on the animal (DC 30). You can choose whether to use this once-per-day effect after the animal misses you.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grim Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1538)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This golden ring is sculpted with the visage of a grinning skull on the side. While wearing the ring, you can attempt to detect the presence of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures as an [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66) activity. This reveals the presence or absence of undead in the area, but it doesn't pinpoint their locations. This ring can't detect undead whose appearance is masked by any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) spell that is 2nd level or higher. If an undead is hiding or disguised, the GM rolls a secret Perception check for you against the undead's [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) or [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC, as appropriate, with a +2 item bonus to your check.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You gain the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition from an undead creature;  **Effect** Reduce the value of the drained condition you gain by 1, and the undead that caused the condition takes 2d6 positive damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L

</row>

---

A horn of the sun aurochs grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks when played as an instrument.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The horn is clearly audible for hundreds of feet, but within a 20-foot emanation, the sound causes agony in those who wish harm upon the user; the horn also sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in that area and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 20 feet. Enemies of the user in this area take 7d6 sonic damage and 7d6 good damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) DC 30 Fortitude save). Creatures that are specifically vulnerable to sunlight are also [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) if they fail this save or frightened 2 on a critical failure. This activation also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) or [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effects in the area (+23 counteract check).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Parade Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1560)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 87

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to medium or heavy armor

</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Parade armor is an armor adjustment that creates a set of pseudo-decorative armor to wear during noncombat functions. Any medium or heavy armor can be fashioned into parade armor, which features custom designs based on the wearer's affiliation, military branch, or rank. A particular faction typically has one uniform appearance for its set of parade armor. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against creatures of the same affiliation as your parade armor, though the bonus to Intimidation doesn't apply when interacting with creatures of higher rank than you. The armor is slightly bulkier, increasing the Bulk by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Parade Armor

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Busine of Divine Reinforcement](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1566)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 89

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, straight, trumpet-like instrument is made of well-polished brass and adorned with imagery of angels fighting demons or religious symbols. When played as an instrument, the busine generates powerful and harmonious notes and grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You play a few notes on the busine. Evil creatures within 60 feet who hear the notes must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) for 1 round, or 2 rounds on a critical failure; this is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You blow into the busine and conjure a [celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16) with the effects of a 6th-level [_summon celestial_](/Spells.aspx?ID=317) spell, but there are no alignment restrictions to define which celestials you can call. The celestial will fight only evil creatures and leaves after all present evil creatures have been defeated or the spell's duration ends, whichever comes first. You can Sustain the Activation to extend the spell's duration, as normal. If you have a deity and the conjured celestial's alignment is one of your deity's preferred alignments, the effect functions as a normal 6th-level _summon celestial_ spell instead, meaning the celestial will fight any creature, not just evil creatures, and will perform other tasks as you command.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Busine of Divine Reinforcement

This sturdy leather saddle was designed to improve communication between mount and rider, allowing even inexperienced knights to command their mounts in battle. A swiftmount saddle grants a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks made to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) for anyone who is riding the creature wearing it.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're adjacent to the creature wearing the _swiftmount saddle_ and could [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=96) it; **Effect** You Mount the creature wearing the _swiftmount saddle_.
