<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hoax-Hunter's Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1616)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 63

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden box unfolds into a stack of hinged trays holding calipers, magnifying lenses, acids and reagents, comparison sketches of species commonly mistaken for one another, and glass slides for specimen testing. When you use this kit to study accounts of a creature or what it left behind (such as spoor, tracks, or fur samples), you gain a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the creature or to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the creature. In addition, if you fail to Recall Knowledge about the creature (but don't critically fail), you're able to eliminate at least one possibility of a common type of animal. For instance, you might be able to verify the creature isn't an [owlbear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=328), even if you get no further information.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Taleteller's Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1648)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 161

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn ring

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Smiling faces are inscribed about this silver band. You become a fluent weaver of fictions, gaining a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47), Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks for storytelling. Whenever you're under suspicion or being questioned by an authority figure, you find yourself compulsively spinning absurd, tall tales that are so unconvincing that they make you look guilty even when you're innocent. The ring's bonuses vanish, and any listener can quickly determine you're lying. Nevertheless, you're completely unable to be honest in such situations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Self-Emptying Pocket](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1656)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You never perform to an empty crowd after promising any profits to a phantom in a playhouse, sealed by holes that appear in your pockets. By meeting with the attendees at your events, you can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49). When you would use Performance to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), you don't earn any gold pieces, as the money disappears before you can even count it.

 The entity that holds your _bargained contract_ can influence your reception any time you perform for a crowd or Activate the pocket. If they influence your performance favorably, you gain a +2 item bonus to your Performance check. If they influence your performance unfavorably, you gain a -2 item penalty to your Performance check. If you've failed to earn the entity money in quite a while, it typically influences your performance unfavorably.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You exercise all of your charm on a creature, turning a chance meeting into an impromptu performance that commands attention. Attempt a single Performance check against the Perception DC of the creature. On a success, the creature is affected as though by a successful Deception check to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=45). The entity that holds your contract can influence this performance as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Self-Emptying Pocket

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Crown of the Kobold King](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1727)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Crown of the Kobold King</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 114

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Originally, this magical crown was to be known as the _Crown of Toil_—a magic item crafted by the dwarven priest named Gristogar. He intended for the crown to bolster his ability to rule and herald in a new era of a dwarven kingdom that followed the teachings of [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28). Yet Gristogar wasn't equal to his own ambitions, and before he could complete the _Crown of Toil_, his cult died out and he ended his own life. For centuries, the incomplete crown lay dormant, but over that time, fragments of Gristogar's soul infused it—not enough to transform it into a truly intelligent item, but enough to give it a rudimentary need and desire to be completed.

 When the Kobold King Merlokrep discovered the discarded crown and placed it upon his head, the crown at last had agency— if not in a hale and hearty dwarf, at least in the frame of a powerful and ambitious kobold. Just as the crown began influencing Merlokrep, so too did the Kobold King's personality influence the crown. Today, the device is known as the _Crown of the Kobold King_, and while it still urges the one who wears it to build a kingdom of endlessly toiling workers, it no longer does so in honor of Droskar. Instead, it seeks the rise of a brutal kobold kingdom.

 The crown appears as a golden band surrounded by iron spikes that glisten with fresh blood—the addition of a kobold skull to the top is largely decorative, but one the crown has grown to “enjoy.” Even in its incomplete state, a few rituals away from its true potential, the _Crown of the Kobold King_ is a potent item. Wearing the crown grants you a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The crown infuses you with the glorious conviction that you're meant to rule, granting you a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, saving throws, and Charisma-based skill checks for 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast a [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Crown of the Kobold King</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

**Base Weapon** [Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=4)_ appears little more than a dull, soot-stained, metal-plated glove at first glance, but in truth it is an invasive boon granted by [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28) to his most faithful subjects. Placing the glove on your hand causes excruciating pain as your appendage curls into a tight fist and then slowly transforms to a supernaturally hard ball of black stone, fusing to you and preventing you from using this hand for anything other than gauntlet Strikes or the _grasp of Droskar's_ activated abilities (a _grasp of Droskar_ does not have the [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=181) weapon trait). The gauntlet can't be removed without a successful casting of [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or a similar spell. The gauntlet reverts to normal (and can be removed with ease) if the curse is removed, the hand is removed from the body, or the wearer dies.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain a +1 item bonus on all Athletics checks for 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_ray of enfeeblement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) with a spell attack modifier of +10 and a DC of 20.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grasp of Droskar
search_markdown:

item_bonus_note: Checks made to resolve the effects of a ritual with the divination trait
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Heartripper Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1730)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Crown of the Kobold King</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 115

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wicked-looking curved weapon is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ that draws power from defeating foes, either to bolster itself or its wielder. A _heartripper blade_ is well suited for use in any ritual that involves divination, and incorporating it into any ritual with the [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) trait grants the wielder a +1 item bonus on any check made to resolve the effects of the ritual's casting.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You reduce a foe to 0 Hit Points with a Strike from the _heartripper blade_;  **Effect** The _heartripper blade_ draws power from the devastating strike, causing its blade to erupt in black flames. For 1 minute, Strikes with the _heartripper blade_ inflict an additional 1d6 negative damage to living creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You reduce a foe to 0 Hit Points with a Strike from the _heartripper blade_;  **Effect** The _heartripper blade_ draws life from the victim's body and siphons it into you. Choose one of the following effects: restore 2d8 Hit Points; reduce the value of your [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions by 1; or reduce the stage of one toxin or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) you're suffering by one stage (this can't reduce the stage below stage 1 or cure the affliction).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heartripper Blade

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs=""
>
[Oculus of Abaddon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1747)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Kingmaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 587

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _oculus of Abaddon_ is a potent artifact rumored to have been created by one of the [Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=11) as a way to reward those who work as their agents on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). More likely, these items were crafted by a powerful pre-Earthfall death cult that has long since moved on, leaving behind these dangerous artifacts to tempt mortals into serving the needs of the Horsemen. 

An _oculus of Abaddon_ appears as a sphere of clear crystal that contains a pinpoint of flickering red light at its center. When held, the oculus feels warm to the touch and fills you with a sudden desire to pluck out an eye and place the oculus within the socket—this causes 4d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). Once placed in an eye socket, the oculus can be removed only by ripping it free (causing the same amount of persistent bleed damage as the initial plucking). When you place the oculus in your empty eye socket, you immediately heal all damage caused by plucking the previous eye out. Once placed, an oculus grants you constant [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). It can also be activated to perform a number of other magical effects, as detailed below, but if you aren't evil, each time you activate an _oculus of Abaddon_, you must succeed at a DC 36 Will save or become affected by a [_subconscious suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) (heightened to 8th level) to perform an evil act the next time an opportunity to do so arises, as determined by the GM.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision);  **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You gain the effects of [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268), heightened to 7th level.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You use the _oculus of Abaddon_ to cast a [_planar binding_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=17) ritual, heightened to 7th level. You can only conjure neutral evil creatures with this casting of _planar binding_. If you already know the _planar binding_ ritual, you gain a +3 item bonus on the primary check when casting _planar binding_.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision;    **Requirements** you have a [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=160) or [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=149); **Effect** You can see and hear through your familiar or animal companion's eyes and ears, provided you are on the same plane. This effect can be sustained, but as long as you do so you can take no other actions.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 hour (envision; [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) **Frequency** once per year;   **Effect** You manipulate the minds of a huge number of targets, provided that the end goal of the manipulation is a tragic or otherwise horrific fate for those being manipulated. This functions as [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), but with a range of 1 mile. All creatures within this area are affected, with the same suggestion implanted in their minds. Creatures hear the telepathic suggestion in their native language, and creatures that are 7th level or higher can attempt a DC 36 Will save to resist the effect. This power was used by Vordakai to cause the vanishing of Varnhold, suggesting to its inhabitants to leave their homes and travel south to his tomb. If this effect is [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) or removed on one victim, it ends for all victims.

**Destruction** A foul and hideous object, an _oculus of Abaddon_ is fortunately relatively easy to destroy, as far as artifacts go. It has Hardness 25 and 100 Hit Points, but repairs damage to itself swiftly, regaining 10 Hit Points per round. It has weakness 10 to bludgeoning damage and weakness 30 to good damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oculus of Abaddon

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ring of the Tiger](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1750)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Kingmaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 589

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ring, carved from green wood, bears carvings of tigers chasing each other through grasslands and forests. _Rings of the tiger_ are traditionally crafted by Kellids in northeastern Avistan, and are often worn by scouts or explorers. Many barbarians in these regions react poorly to anyone obviously not part of their people who dare to wear such a ring, but ironically have been known to gift these rings to outsiders who have proven their worth—potentially setting up a further clash in the future should that character encounter another group of Kellids who aren't familiar with how the wearer earned the right to wear the ring. A ring of the tiger grants a +1 item bonus on [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and Perception checks made in outdoor wilderness areas, and it can be activated to momentarily transform the wearer's hand into a tiger's claw.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** You aren't carrying anything in the hand on which the ring is worn; **Effect** Your hand becomes a tiger's claw, granting you an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) claw [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that inflicts 1d6 slashing damage, and you Strike with the claw. Then the claw turns back to normal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Limited"
>
Ring of the Tiger 
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 589

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Ring of the Tiger (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 589

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the claw inflicts 2d6 slashing damage; the additional damage die is not cumulative with additional damage dice from _[striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ runes.
name: Ring of the Tiger

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stag's Helm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1752)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Kingmaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 589

**Price** 240 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This impressive-looking helmet is crafted to resemble the skull of a mighty stag. Although made from bone, the antlers and helm are as strong as iron. While the Stag Lord himself wears this helmet in the Kingmaker Adventure Path, _stag's helms_ are actually the creation of the church of [Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=6), and worshipers of this deity find that the helm is not only particularly comfortable to wear, but that they can activate it once per hour rather than once per day.

 Rather than restrict the senses, a _stag's helm_ enhances them, causing the world to appear in more vibrant colors and significantly enhancing the ambient sounds of the region without making them distracting. While worn, it grants a +1 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" />  ;  **Frequency** once per day (or once per hour if the wearer worships Erastil);   **Effect** Choose a single creature within 30 feet. The _stag's helm_ focuses your aim and grants supernatural insight into your next shot. Your target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the next ranged Strike you make this turn against them.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stag's Helm

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Palette of Masterstrokes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1759)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Kingmaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 84

**Price** 1,950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This painter's palette is decorated with an image of [Shelyn's](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) religious symbol: a songbird with a colorful curved tail. The feathers on this tail constantly produce a wide range of colorful pigments, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks made to create paintings. A _palette of masterstrokes_ functions as a divine focus for a cleric of Shelyn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _palette of masterstrokes_ casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=54) as a 5th-level divine spell to your specifications.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** With a sweeping motion, you swing the _palette of masterstrokes_ in your hand. It casts [_color spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=44) as a 4th-level divine spell. The spell DC is 29.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Performance checks made to dance or sing as long as the glaive is held in two hands
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Songbird's Brush](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1760)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Kingmaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 84

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ shimmers with a prismatic sheen, as if faintly reflecting a rainbow. A _songbird's brush_ sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 40 feet). When wielded in combat, the sound of the blade cutting through the air creates a soft, musical trill as if produced by a songbird. Sacred to the faithful of [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=17), a _songbird's brush_ is so named for the fighting style developed by worshippers of the Eternal Rose, which equates its sweeping swings to painting with a brush. A songbird's brush grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks made to dance or sing as long as the glaive is held in two hands.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You sweep the _songbird's brush_ in an arc before you, casting [_prismatic spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) from the glaive. The spell DC is 34. This _prismatic spray_ does not affect good-aligned creatures in its area.

**Craft Requirements** You are a worshipper of Shelyn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Songbird's Brush

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Curtain Call Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1782)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blue velvet cloak is cut from the remains of a curtain from a destroyed theater. When worn, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while acting, orating, or singing, as the cloak makes minor motions to accentuate your performance.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take a bow, spread your arms, and the cloak casts 4th-level [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) centered on a corner of your space. This darkness doesn't impede your vision. While the darkness persists, it emits noise like the applauding of a moderately sized crowd.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Curtain Call Cloak

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Spectral Opera Glasses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1784)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bulky opera glasses are attached to a rod for ease of use. When held up to the eyes, which requires a free hand, you can see up to four times further than normal, and the glasses provide you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 hour, anyone who looks through the glasses can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures and objects, and sees creatures with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) trait as solid and substantial (rather than ghostly).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectral Opera Glasses

item_bonus_note: Charisma-based skill checks while within the red light
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Blood Lords **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78 **Price** 1,600 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands or mounted **Bulk** 3 --- This drum-shaped metal floodlight has a metal handle on each side and a housing at its base to be slotted into a stand or pedestal. A _splendid floodlight_ has a primary function as well as several secondary functions available by rotating through a series of translucent sheets called gels. A _splendid floodlight_ comes with the four gels described below, but other gels exist with different abilities. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The _splendid floodlight's_ light is off; **Effect** You flip a switch and the _splendid floodlight_ emits bright magical light in a 120-foot cone that hangs in the air and remains in place even if you move the floodlight. If the cone passes through an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the splendid floodlight attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness. Creatures in the cone who are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) reduce the value of their frightened condition by 1 at the beginning of each turn they start in the area of the cone. The light remains in place until you Interact to flip the switch again to extinguish the floodlight. If you haven't done so after an hour, the switch flips on its own and the floodlight turns off. **Activate** Interact; **Requirements** The _splendid floodlight's_ light is on; **Effect** You rotate one of the following gels into position in the floodlight or rotate a gel out. You can do this even if the _splendid floodlight_ has been moved from its position when the light was created. You can have up to two gels in place at once; if you rotate in a third, another of your choice rotates out.
  • **Red Gel** The light lends an air of gravitas to all creatures illuminated by it. Creatures in the light gain a +2 item bonus to Charisma-based skill checks.
  • **Yellow Gel** [Invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures and objects are visible while in the light, and creatures with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) trait appear substantial rather than ghostly.
  • **Green Gel** All creatures illuminated by the light appear to be wearing exceptionally stylish clothing with elegantly coiffed hair styles.
  • **Blue Gel** [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures in the light appear as they did when alive, and living creatures in the light appear decayed, like an undead creature.
name: Splendid Floodlight

item_bonus_note: Disable a Device checks made against clockwork creatures
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wrenchgear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1791)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 106

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Alkenstar have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The ubiquity of clockwork constructs in Alkenstar spurred the development of the wrenchgear (a shortened “wrench in the gears”) by innovative criminals (“wrenchers”) to create security exploits and larcenous opportunities. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) checks made against [clockwork](/Traits.aspx?ID=331) creatures (typically to wind them down). 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wrenchgear

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You're about to attempt a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect or an effect that would make you [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6);  **Effect** Abraxas envelops your heart in the shadow of his secret, granting you a +2 status bonus on your saving throw against the triggering effect.

[Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=71) teaches that an eye for an eye is the basis of law. This tattoo of a coiled viper provides a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** Abraxas transforms the tattoo into a weapon of your vengeance. The _vengeful arm_ crawls into your hand and becomes a [retribution axe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=393). You can use this activation again while holding the axe to revert the vengeful arm to a tattoo. Like other _retribution axes_, you can etch fundamental runes into the _vengeful arm's_ _retribution axe_ to increase its potency or to add [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs=""
>
[Shadewither Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1818)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized talisman hangs on a string of braided bark fibers. It resembles a leaf from some unknown tree similar to oak, carved from jade and then dipped halfway into black ink that looks wet despite feeling completely dry. The gate key was originally created to open the aiudara known as the _Seventh Arch_. In recent years, corrupting magic has given it additional, stranger powers.

 When you wear and invest _the Shadewither Key_, shadows gather around you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. However, this boon comes with great risk: any [elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) who touches _the Shadewither Key_ is immediately afflicted with the [_obnubilate curse_](/Curses.aspx?ID=49).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You touch _the Shadewither Key_ and command the shadows it casts around you to peel away. The shadows form an umbral double that resembles you in every way except for its fiery red eyes and the expression of wicked delight on its face. The double occupies the same space as you and attempts to intercept any attacks aimed at you. A creature must succeed at a DC 11 flat check when targeting you with an attack, spell, or other effect; on a failure, they hit the shadowy double instead of you. The effect lasts for 1 minute or until the double is hit, whichever comes first. Once the double is hit, it's destroyed. As long as the double exists, you don't gain _the Shadewither Key's_ item bonus to Stealth checks.

**Destruction** _The Shadewither Key's_ magic can be destroyed by exposing the artifact to uncontained teleportation energies. If touched to an unlinked or malfunctioning _aiudara_, _the Shadewither Key_ causes the gate to connect to a new counterpart. If this happens, the key's magic leaves it, rendering the item useless.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadewither Key

These black leather goggle frames have no lenses. Instead, when a character puts them on and invests them, the wearer's eyes transform and lengthen into snail-like eyestalks. The wobbly eyestalks stretch out through the lens holes and up over the wearer's head on lengthened optic nerves.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By focusing hard, you can watch for enemies in all directions. You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute; during this time, you can't be flanked.

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather goggle frames have no lenses. Instead, when a character puts them on and invests them, the wearer's eyes transform and lengthen into snail-like eyestalks. The wobbly eyestalks stretch out through the lens holes and up over the wearer's head on lengthened optic nerves.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By focusing hard, you can watch for enemies in all directions. You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute; during this time, you can't be flanked.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bastion of the Inheritor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1841)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Worn by [Iomedae's](/Deities.aspx?ID=9) prestigious knights, this _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is emblazoned with the Inheritor's [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) and sports a white cloak. While the cloak is white, this armor grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), provided your target has no enmity toward Iomedae.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The armor's cloak becomes red for 1 minute. As long as the cloak is red, you gain the benefits of the armor's deflect melee trait without needing to spend an additional action to activate it during each turn.

**Craft Requirements** You worship Iomedae.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bastion of the Inheritor

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trollhound Vest](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1854)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 19

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _+1 [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)_ is sickly green and covered in thick warts and nodules, fashioned from the hide of a [trollhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835) and imbued with some of the beast's vitality. Wearing this armor gives you a –1 circumstance penalty to all checks made using [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to interact with [trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=395) and a +1 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks used to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) in communities traditionally plagued by troll attacks.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** You take damage from a melee attack while you have half or fewer of your normal maximum Hit Points;  **Effect** Your body knits itself back together, healing you for 3d8 Hit Points.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hides of at least two [trollhounds](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Trollhound Vest

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helmsman's Recourse](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1858)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Meteor Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [standard-grade duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2815) [meteor shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13) (Hardness 7, HP 28, BT 14) is a wheel from a ship. While wielding the shield, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You're in a body of water; **Effect** For 10 minutes, you don't sink if you haven't succeeded at a Swim action on a turn, and if you're submerged, you automatically ascend 10 feet at the end of your turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 60, and BT 30, and when you Activate the shield, it casts [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) on you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 22

**Price** 2,650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 11, HP 80, and BT 40; the item bonus is +2; and when you Activate the shield, you can breathe underwater for 10 minutes.
name: Helmsman's Recourse

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Auric Noodles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1909)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Auric noodles are boiled, then pan-fried and tossed with sliced vegetables and a sticky, savory sauce incorporating alchemical reagents. After you eat the noodles, they boost your ability to sense magic for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), and you can move at full speed while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=513) exploration activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Auric Noodles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cinnamon Seers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1910)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Zippy, alchemically treated cinnamon suffuses cinnamon seers, a rock candy with a lively taste that provides a mental boost. A cinnamon seer remains in your mouth for 1 hour, its stimulating flavor granting you a +1 item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26).

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Trigger** You gain no information from a Recall Knowledge check; **Effect** Reroll the triggering check. If this was a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check, the GM rerolls rather than you; the candy doesn't give you any insight into what the GM rolled, so in that case, you're rerolling based only on guesswork. The seer becomes inert. You become temporarily immune to cinnamon seers until the next time you make your daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cinnamon Seers

If fried, cast-off scales of a [benthic worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2872) render into crunchy snacks. [Alchemists](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) add reagents to the frying oil to enhance the scales’ properties and flavor. For 1 minute after eating a delve scale, you gain a burrow Speed of 15 feet and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diplomat's Charcuterie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1914)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 47

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A common sight at political gatherings, a diplomat's charcuterie has fine meats, cheeses, nuts, fruits, and other finger foods mixed with reagents to engender friendly feelings between those consuming them. Contents of the plate vary by chef and the intended palates, from the hearty sausages and hard cheese of charcuterie from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to the hot-pepper cheese curds and smoked almonds of Thuvian platters. After Activating the charcuterie by eating it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) and Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85). These bonuses last 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diplomat's Charcuterie

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 48 **Price** 27 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Containing neither eggs nor cream, an egg cream fizz contains milk or nut milk, sparkling water, and flavored syrup, frothed and chilled. Upon drinking, you feel lighter and more buoyant, gaining a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed for 10 minutes. During this time, you also gain another effect determined by the drink's syrup, which is chosen when the drink is created.
  • **Chocolate** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28), [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30), and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31).
  • **Strawberry** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).
  • **Vanilla** You can Step into difficult terrain.
name: Egg Cream Fizz

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 49 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- A popular choice for [investigators](/Classes.aspx?ID=13) studying alchemy, insight coffee is infused with alchemical flavoring during percolation. For 1 hour after you drink an insight coffee, you use d8s instead of d6s for your extra damage from the strategic strike class feature, if you have it. You also gain an item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge with a skill determined by the blend chosen when the item is crafted.
  • **Double Coffee** [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)
  • **Hazelnut** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)
  • **Mocha** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)
  • **Vanilla** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)
  • **Pumpkin Spice** [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)
  • **Toffee** [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

Journeybread contains a mix of fruits, nuts, and grains with an alchemical boost. Eating one journeybread provides all the food and water you need for a day. If you subsist on nothing else for a week, you become temporarily immune to journeybread until you eat real food and drink water normally for 24 hours.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hot, broth-based soup typically contains tubers, leeks, zesty spices, reagents, and if desired, the meat of livestock. Civic authorities commission batches of mender's soup for workers if a tricky job is on the agenda. After you eat the soup, its effects last 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) and restore an additional 5 Hit Points to items you successfully Repair during this time. 

If you eat mender's soup over the entire period required to attempt a Crafting check to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) (typically a minimum of eating the soup each day for 4 days), the +1 bonus from the soup can be applied to that check, too.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mender's Soup

---

A phantom roll contains vegetables, greens, and fine, clear noodles, all wrapped in transparent, edible starch paper and alchemically treated and laced with a tangy sauce. Upon eating the roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you attempt during the [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=511) exploration activity. You can also Avoid Notice at full Speed or combine it with [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) or [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=518) while moving at half Speed. These effects expire 24 hours after you eat the roll or when you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Roll

**Bulk** L

---

A sparkling candy coating covers a sprite apple. For 10 minutes after consuming a sprite apple, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). While shedding this light, you can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if you're visible, and if you're [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you're concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The light matches the vibrant color of the apple's candy coating. Creatures in the bright light are subject to another effect, depending on the type of apple.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tracker's Stew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1933)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 51

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Alchemical reagents add punch to tracker's stew: a rich, fiery mixture of tomatoes, ground nuts, onions, and tubers, often with poultry added. It's usually served with or over rice or noodles. Once you've eaten the stew, it improves your ability to sense and follow tracks for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66). You can do either while moving at full speed or both while moving at half speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bendy-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1958)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack damage.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bendy-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1958)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack damage.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Athletics checks to Shove and Trip
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deadweight Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1961)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). 

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) condition and can't remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour. You can attempt to Shove or Trip creatures up to two sizes larger than you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). 

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) condition and can't remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

 **Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. If you're untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to Perception checks and Will saves. After any round where you don't spend at least 1 action to Interact with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59), Step, or Stride, you're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +1, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

 **Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. If you're untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to Perception checks and Will saves. After any round where you don't spend at least 1 action to Interact with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59), Step, or Stride, you're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

 **Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. If you're untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to Perception checks and Will saves. After any round where you don't spend at least 1 action to Interact with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59), Step, or Stride, you're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />

<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

</row>

---

The faceplate of a dread helm has a fierce visage that magnifies the effects of fear. Concealed within is a reservoir that can hold a single [dread ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3292), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A dread ampoule is installed in the helm; **Effect** The dread ampoule atomizes, creating a fear‑inducing mist that hangs around your face for 3 rounds. The mist grants an item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks equal to the dread ampoule’s item bonus. The mist also deals mental damage equal to the dread ampoule’s splash damage to all creatures within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) other than you. The activation uses up the dread ampoule, and the helm can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This suit of [leaf weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28) armor is specially modified to metabolize the alchemical accelerants in medicinal compounds. A special receptacle in the armor can hold an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** An elixir of life is installed in the armor; **Effect** Slithering vines grow from the armor, granting an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), to your Fortitude DC to resist Grapple, [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) attempts, and to your Reflex DC to resist Trip attempts. The bonus is equal to the elixir’s item bonus, and lasts for 3 rounds. The activation uses up the elixir, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

</column>

This [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41) has been treated with extract from [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), which can be reactivated in the presence of a strong acid. A receptacle in the armor can hold an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** An acid flask is installed in the armor; **Effect** The leather weeps slippery protoplasm, granting an item bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) checks equal to the acid flask’s item bonus. The protoplasm also irritates the skin on prolonged contact, causing any creature that [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [swallows](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) you to take acid damage equal to the acid flask’s splash damage. Ooze skin remains activated for a number of rounds equal to the level of the acid flask installed. The activation uses up the acid flask, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

Tradition says the _dragonfly fulu_ should be affixed to the upper back, like the wings of an insect. When you Activate this fulu, you gain a +2 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute. During this time, you can attempt an Athletics check to High Jump or Long Jump as a single action without the Stride requirement. You can also High Jump or Long Jump from a nonsolid substance, such as air or water, but if you use this power of the fulu, its effects ends after you jump.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

---

When you drink sweet, sky-blue _Elysian dew_, for 1 minute, you gain a 10-foot aura that evokes the vitality of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16), causing nearby objects to seem more colorful and plants to stand taller. You and any ally that starts its turn in the emanation gain 5 temporary Hit Points, a +1 item bonus to saving throws, and a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks until the start of your or the ally's next turn. If you're evil and drink this potion, it fails to work, and you must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or the potion renders you [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bargainer's Instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2116)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 96

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 hour ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bargainer’s instrument_ is a musical implement. The most typical version is a violin made from maple and spruce, its body stained so deep a purple it appears almost black. When activated, the instrument casts a [_binding circle_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110) ritual to conjure a willing extraplanar talent to compete with you in performance.

The contest could be a musical battle, a dance-off, an oratorical debate—the two of you could even use entirely different types of performance. The loser of the bout is magically bound to perform a task of the winner’s choice within 1 week. The contest consists of taking turns to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), starting with your opponent. The Performance check’s DC is the higher of the opponent’s [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) DC or Will DC, though the two parties can mutually agree to use an impartial judge instead. The first to reach four successes wins, with each critical successes counting as two successes. If you play the bargainer’s instrument as part of your performance, it grants you a +2 item bonus to the Performance checks.

After the contest, the conjured creature can choose to return to where it came from or remain where you conjured it. The loser must endeavor to complete the task by all reasonable means within 1 week. Failing to complete it is shameful but has no further consequences. The instrument loses its magic and remains a non-magical [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bargainer's Instrument

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Artificer Spectacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2134)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 102

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These seemingly ordinary rectangular eyeglasses feature clear lenses framed in copper. When invested and worn, they rest perfectly on the bridge of the nose and can only be removed by the wearer. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks and any skill check made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). When you invest the spectacles, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _artificer spectacles_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-rank [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) spell on an item you touch.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Artificer Spectacles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Avalanche Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2135)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 102

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While the insides of these boots are comfortable, fur-lined leather, the outsides are a jumble of slate plates, giving the impression of a rockslide. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38). When you invest the boots, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a Shove;  **Effect** If the Shove was a success, you push your opponent up to 10 feet instead of 5 feet. If the Shove was a critical success, you push your opponent up to 20 feet, and you can then choose to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Avalanche Boots

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Handwraps](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2138)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 103

**Price** 80,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These silken handwraps feature intricate embroidery of a serpentine red dragon adorned with golden thread. The handwraps function as _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_. You also gain a +4 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks made to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38). When you invest the handwraps, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You slap the bottom of your palms with hands splayed outward, casting a 7th-rank [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) spell (DC 41).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a Grapple;  **Effect** You gain a +2 status bonus to your Athletics DC against any checks made to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) your grapple until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Handwraps

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Laurel of the Empath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2139)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 103

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn circlet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silvery, woven ring of leaves sits on top of the head. While wearing it, when you roll Perception for initiative, you can roll twice and take the higher result. This is a fortune effect. Whenever you spend at least 1 minute talking with a living creature, you automatically become aware of its attitude toward you. When you invest the laurel, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You're hit by an attack;  **Effect** You take half damage from the triggering attack.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail, but don't critically fail, a saving throw;  **Effect** You anticipate the danger and guard against it, often in unconventional or almost inconceivable ways. Treat the failed saving throw as a success.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You spend 10 minutes talking with one living creature, conversing in inspirational, religious, or philosophical terms. You gain valuable insights into the personality of your target—their hopes, dreams, and fears. When the ritual is over, you gain a +4 item bonus to all Perception checks made concerning the target for one month. Also, the target gains inspirational insight, allowing the target to use one of the two reactions listed above once during the next 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Laurel of the Empath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pilferer's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2141)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of soft and subtle black leather, these gloves fit tightly but aren't uncomfortable and don't impede your sense of touch. As long as you're trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) while wearing these gloves, you're always considered one skill rank higher than your actual rank. If you possess a Legendary skill rank in Thievery, you gain a +2 item bonus to Thievery checks instead. When you invest the gloves, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a Thievery skill check;  **Effect** If you failed the Thievery skill check, you succeed at that check instead. If you critically failed, you fail instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pilferer's Gloves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sandals of the Stag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2142)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sturdy leather sandals feature straps that wrap up to the knees. Etched in the leather are intricate patterns of stags leaping through the forest. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your land Speed and a +3 item bonus on [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks when attempting to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). When you invest the sandals, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You attempt a High Jump or Long Jump but you didn't Stride at least 10 feet;  **Effect** You can attempt the jump normally. It doesn't automatically fail.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sandals of the Stag
