<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shadowmist Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2143)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black cape flows more like a vaporous liquid than fabric. The cape grants you a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. When you invest the cape, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value
.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature misses you with an attack;  **Effect** Attempt a Stealth check against the triggering creature's Perception DC. If you roll a success, you're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from that creature until the end of your next turn or until right after you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against that creature. If you roll a critical success, you're hidden from that creature until the end of your next turn, even if you use hostile actions against that creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a twirl of the cape, you transform yourself into a puff of gray smoke. You cast [_vapor form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) on yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadowmist Cape

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whispering Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2146)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 105

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wooden staff is carved with humanoid faces in various emotional states. When the staff is activated, the faces begin to whisper a variety of languages in sibilant tones, creating what seems to be nonsense to all but the staff's wielder or those they choose to affect. The staff functions as a [_major staff of the unblinking eye_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3048). While using the staff, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) checks, regardless of the skill. When you invest the staff, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _whispering staff_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Through the staff's strange whispering, you gain a glimpse into the mind and desires of one creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the end of your next turn, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against your spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You point the staff at one creature you can see within 30 feet of you, causing the whispers to howl in that creature's mind. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. If it fails, whenever the creature attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw, it must roll twice and take the worse roll. This lasts until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twirl the staff in three consecutive circles and call for the whispers to speak up. For the next minute, you and all allies within a 30-foot emanation around you can hear your staff's whispers clearly and distinctly, gaining benefit from their advice and mental protection. Whenever you and your affected allies attempt to Recall Knowledge or attempt a saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect, you roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whispering Staff

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Confabulator](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2166)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 109

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to instrument

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This device attaches to any non-percussive musical instrument, allowing a non-verbal character to shape the sounds of the instrument into speech. The speech can be any language the character understands, and the sound of the speech resembles the instrument the device is attached to. You also gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks made with the instrument.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Confabulator

The target is temporarily immune to your Battle Medicine for 1 hour instead of 1 day.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bring Me Near](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2188)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 114

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This collapsible [fine spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=54) consists of 3 leather tubes that slide into one another. The edge of each is trimmed in silver, and the lenses are made of finely crafted glass. While looking through it, you gain a +2 item bonus to any Perception checks made involving sight.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on any spot you can see within 5 miles through the spyglass and rotate its parts in a meticulous order. You and up to 4 willing creatures adjacent to you are instantly teleported to that spot. If there's not enough room for everyone, only you are transported. If there's not enough room for you, the teleportation fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bring Me Near

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ancestral Geometry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2202)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Geometric precision and perfect angles signify that an artist with exemplary knowledge of [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) tattooing traditions created this body art. Your family's ancestral stories, recited throughout the tattooing process, bind your blood even tighter to theirs. During your daily preparations, you manifest a visitation by an ancestor—possibly via a dream, a vision, or a magical trinket left by your bedroll. Roll 2d20 and record the highest result. Then roll 1d6 and note a type of saving throw: 1–2 Fortitude, 3–4 Reflex, and 5–6 Will.

 In addition, until the next visitation, you gain a +1 item bonus to one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill related to the ancestor, as determined by the GM. Usually, the ancestor provides a kind of knowledge they believe you'll need. For dwarven ancestors, the Lore skill is usually Architecture Lore, Engineering Lore, Genealogy Lore, Labor Lore, Mining Lore, Warfare Lore, or Lore about a dwarven deity.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You rolled a saving throw of the noted type;  **Effect** Replace the roll with the d20 roll from your ancestor's visitation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ancestral Geometry

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small scars or marks around your eye improve your distant vision. These scars are especially common among [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) scouts, who favor scars shaped like eagle talons. You can see four times farther than normal. If you have [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), you can see blood in color. Higher-level versions of an _eye slash_ are larger and more elaborate scars or marks, radiating out around the eye.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eye Slash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2206)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small scars or marks around your eye improve your distant vision. These scars are especially common among [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) scouts, who favor scars shaped like eagle talons. You can see four times farther than normal. If you have [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), you can see blood in color. Higher-level versions of an _eye slash_ are larger and more elaborate scars or marks, radiating out around the eye.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[One Hundred Victories](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2214)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 121

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One hundred cuts, healed into diamond-shaped scars, represent the ability to withstand the attacks of your enemies. [Orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) warriors covet these scar patterns and cluster them around what they consider to be their strongest assets—a pattern around the heart signifies a warrior with great endurance, while one along the arms indicates great upper body strength. 

You gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks. If you have [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16) (such as from the [Orc Ferocity](/Feats.aspx?ID=83) feat or a similar ability), the first time each day you use it, instead of remaining at 1 Hit Point, your Hit Points are set to an amount equal to your ancestry Hit Points, even if this amount is more than you had before using Ferocity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: One Hundred Victories

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2218)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Placed under the eyes like rays of light, this tattoo burns away illusions with the unmerciful brilliance of the sun. You gain a +2 bonus to Perception checks that involve sight. If you're [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), you receive a new save at the start of each of your turns to end your dazzled condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts a 4th-rank [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), with the rays emitting from your eyes. The number of actions you spend Activating the tattoo determines _blazing bolt’s_ number of rays. The tattoo also attempts to dispel each illusion on a creature hit by a ray ([counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank 5th, counteract modifier +19).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Sight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tradecraft Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2220)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafters choose tattoos that represent their dedication and skill in their chosen field. Such tattoos might adorn the arm, fingers, or eyes, and they take the form of artistic patterns or depict tools of the trade, such as anvils, paintbrushes, or trowels. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks. Furthermore, when you roll a critical failure on a Crafting check to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), treat it as a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=54). You choose the item and its appearance, and whether the spell is 4th or 5th rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tradecraft Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tradecraft Tattoo (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Crafting checks is +3. The _creation_ spell's duration is unlimited but ends when you Activate the tattoo again.
name: Tradecraft Tattoo

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Triangular Teeth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2221)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 33 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rows of triangles symbolizing shark teeth protect you from danger and enable you to take fierce retaliation against those who try to harm you. Seafarers, especially those on the seas of Minata, wear these tattoos in patterns, with multiple rows of regular triangles. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to navigate bodies of water.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would be hit by an attack against your AC;  **Effect** You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the attack, or a +2 circumstance bonus if the attacker is in water or has the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207), [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait. Whether the attack hits or misses, you gain a +2 status bonus to damage with the next Strike you make against the attacker before the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Triangular Teeth

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** tattooed on the body --- The name of your beloved adorns this stylized image of a heart. An _unbreakable heart_ tattoo can be received only at the end of a successful [_heartbond_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=33) ritual, serving as your token. The other participant can have a token other than a tattoo if they wish. If you have more than one heartbond, each _unbreakable heart_ you have serves as a token for only one of them. The love exuding from you grants you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. When you use _heartbond's_ activity to learn your beloved's present state, you can also grant them a small gift, choosing from the following options each time. Make your choice after learning their state.
  • Your beloved gains 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 10 minutes.
  • The tattoo casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) on your beloved's token from _heartbond_.
name: Unbreakable Heart
